REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER BY DEFENDANT JACKIE LACEY, IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS LOS ANGELES COUNTY DISTRICT ATTORNEY, TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFFS ROBERT BRODY, M.D., ET AL. (TRANSACTION ID # 16020025) FILED BY DEFENDANT LACEY, JACKIE IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS DISTRICT ATTORNEY FOR LOS ANGELES COUNTY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KAMALA HARRIS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IT APPEARS THAT THE CONSTITUTIONAL ISSUES RAISED HAVE BEEN DECIDED BY THE COURTS OF APPEAL IN CALIFORNIA IN DONALDSON V. LUNGREN AND DONOROVICH-O'DONNELL V. HARRIS. THE COURT NOTES THAT IN DONALDSON V. LUNGREN A DEMURRER WAS AT ISSUE AND SUSTAINED. THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT THERE IS NO INDIVIDUAL SEEKING RELIEF. THEORETICALLY, A DOCTOR MAY BE FACED WITH A REQUEST TO ASSIST AT ENDING LIFE, BUT THAT IS NOT ON THE TABLE RIGHT NOW. THE COURT FINDS THIS CASE IS NOT RIPE, FINDING NO ACTUAL CASE IN CONTROVERSY. SIMILARLY, THE COURT CANNOT IGNORE THE ENACTMENT OF SENATE BILL 181 AND ITS PROGENY OR INCARNATION, WHICH CONSIDERED THE RIGHT TO ASSISTED SUICIDE, ESSENTIALLY PROVIDING THE RESULT PLAINTIFFS SEEK, BUT WITH CAREFULLY CONSIDERED PROTOCOLS, LIMITATIONS, AND RULES. WHILE SB 128 MAY NOT BE IN EFFECT YET, IT MUST BE STRONGLY CONSIDERED, AND IS CONSIDERED BY THIS COURT, GIVEN THE FACT THAT IT SIMPLY MATCHES WHAT THE PLAINTIFFS HAVE BEEN SEEKING, BUT SPEAKS WITH THE AUTHORITY OF THE LEGISLATURE. DEFENDANTS SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 BY NEXT WEEK. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA/SEAN KANE. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Notice Of Joinder In Attorney Generals Demurrer
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Nicholas van Aelstyn of Beveridge & Diamond PC telephone number 415-262-4000 for Plaintiffs.
Kathryn Tucker of Disability Rights Legal Center telephone number 206-595-0097 for Plaintiffs.
Darin Wessel of Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP telephone number 619-515-0269 for Defendant Jackie Lacey.
Darrell Spence Deputy Attorney General telephone number 916-323-8988 for Defendant Kamala Harris.

As to DEFENDANT GEORGE GASCON'S Joinder In Attorney Generals Demurrer, see the Court's ruling as to that demurrer. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Sean Kane. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nicholas van Aelstyn of Beveridge & Diamond PC telephone number 415-262-4000 for Plaintiffs.
Kathryn Tucker of Disability Rights Legal Center telephone number 206-595-0097 for Plaintiffs.
Darin Wessel of Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP telephone number 619-515-0269 for Defendant Jackie Lacey.
Darrell Spence Deputy Attorney General telephone number 916-323-8988 for Defendant Kamala Harris.
Wayne Snodgrass Deputy City Attorney telephone number 415 554-4675 for Defendant George Gascon.

The matter was argued and submitted. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT KAMALA HARRIS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. It appears that the constitutional issues raised have been decided by the Courts of Appeal in California in Donaldson v. Lungren and Donorovich-O'Donnell v. Harris. The Court notes that in Donaldson v. Lungren a demurrer was at issue and sustained. The Court also notes that there is no individual seeking relief. Theoretically, a doctor may be faced with a request to assist at ending life, but that is not on the table right now. The Court finds this case is not ripe, finding no actual case in controversy. Similarly, the Court cannot ignore the enactment of Senate Bill 181 and its progeny or incarnation, which considered the right to assisted suicide, essentially providing the result plaintiffs seek, but with carefully considered protocols, limitations, and rules. While SB 128 may not be in effect yet, it must be strongly considered, and is considered by this Court, given the fact that it simply matches what the plaintiffs have been seeking, but speaks with the authority of the legislature. Defendants shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by next week. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Sean Kane. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. NICHOLAS W. VAN AELSTYN OF BEVERIDGE & DIAMOND, TEL. NO. 1-415-262-4000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. KATRYN TUCKER OF DRLC, TEL. NO. 1-213-736-8195 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. NIROMI W. PFEIFFER & ATTY. DARREN W. SPENCE OF THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, TEL. NO. 1=916-445-4931 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT KAMALA D. HARRIS;
ATTY. DARIN L.WESSEL OF THE MANNING & KASS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-619-515-0271 APPEARING FOR THE
DEFENDANT JACKIE LACEY.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS ROBERT BRODY, MARC CONANT, DONALD ABRAMS, ROBERT LINER, DANIEL SWANGARD, CHRISTIE WHITE AND ANGELICA BLOOMQUIST'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction is DENIED. Plaintiffs seek a preliminary injunction which "recognizes" that Penal Code section 401 does not apply to aid in dying. Plaintiffs assert that their claim is not moot under Bartling. (Bartling v. Superior Court (1984) 163 Cal. App. 3d 186, 197.) Bartling held that the terminally-ill patient's "death renders moot that portion of the petition which seeks an order compelling the superior court to grant the injunction sought." (Ibid.) Even if this court has considerable discretion because the restrictive federal doctrine of justiciability does not apply in state courts, White v. Davis (1975) 13 Cal.3d 757, 762, Bartling is apposite and establishes that plantiffs claim is nonjusticiable. Furthermore, even though state courts are not bound by Article III of the United States Constitution, Asarco Inc. v. Kadish (1989) 490 U.S. 605, 610, the California Supreme Court upholds the "majority rule depriving a derivative plaintiff of standing where, as here, his or her interest in the litigation is completely extinguished. " (Grosset v. Wenaas (2008) 42 Cal. 4th 1100, 1117.) Here, because the terminally ill patient's "own motion is rendered moot by her death." and plaintiffs are unsure whether their new patients "meet the criteria for aid in dying", even under California law it is unlikely that plaintiffs will prevail on the merits because the merits cannot even be reached. Even if this court could meet the merits of this case it is unlikely plaintiff would prevail. Penal Code Section 401 establishes that every person who deliberately aids another to commit suicide, is guilty of a felony. Statutory language should be given effect consistent with its plain meaning and in light of the aim of the legislature unless to do so would achieve an illogical result. (Radzanower v. Touche Ross & Co. (1976) 426 U.S. 148, 166.) Even if the legislature has not provided a statutory definition of suicide, California's ban against assisted suicide contains no exception to its prohibition. This court has received Plaintiffs' Sur-Reply. Plaintiffs do not establish that their case is justiciable on the basis that they are now "willing to explore" with new patients whether "they meet the criteria for aid in dying." Defendant Kamala D. Harris' Objection to Plaintiffs' Sur-Reply in Support of Motion for Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply witn CRC 3.1312. The Request for a Statement of decision is granted. Defendants to prepare a Proposed Statement of Decision. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
